skill.name: typescript-write
skill.description: Write TypeScript and JavaScript code following Metabase coding standards and best practices. Use when developing or refactoring TypeScript/JavaScript code.
skill.summary: Write TypeScript and JavaScript code following Metabase coding standards and best practices. Use whe...
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged inline Yarn command examples and one weak cryptography pattern. The Yarn entries are documentation for linting, formatting, type checking, and tests, while the cryptography alert on the description line has no supporting code evidence. No prompt injection, network access, secret access, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Documented External Project Commands
low_findings.description: The skill lists Yarn commands for linting, formatting, type checking, and tests. These are legitimate developer workflow instructions, but they can execute local project scripts when an AI agent follows them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are direct command examples in Markdown, not hidden executable code. There is still a low operational risk because running Yarn invokes repository-defined scripts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static high-severity alert points to the skill description line. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, encryption code, or credential handling appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain frontmatter text describing TypeScript and JavaScript development guidance. I found no evidence of weak cryptography in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Metabase contributors need code that follows local TypeScript and JavaScript conventions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through tests, linting, formatting, and project-specific workflow habits.
actual_capabilities: Guides TypeScript and JavaScript edits using Metabase coding standards.
Instructs agents to understand existing patterns before implementation.
Encourages failing tests before fixes when behavior changes.
Lists project commands for ESLint, Prettier, type checking, and unit tests.
Supports focused JavaScript, TypeScript, and ClojureScript test workflows.
limitations: It does not provide reusable code snippets or architectural templates.
It assumes the target repository uses the listed Yarn commands.
It does not define rules for backend Clojure changes.
It does not replace human review before commits.

use_cases.title: Refactor Frontend Code
use_cases.description: Improve TypeScript or JavaScript while preserving Metabase conventions and validating changes with targeted checks.

use_cases.title: Fix Failing Tests
use_cases.description: Use a test-first workflow to reproduce a defect, implement a focused fix, and rerun relevant unit tests.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reviewable Changes
use_cases.description: Format, lint, type check, and leave changes ready for a user-managed commit and review process.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Small TypeScript Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to update this TypeScript file. First inspect nearby patterns, then make the smallest safe change and run relevant checks.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Regression Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to reproduce this bug with a failing test, implement the fix, and run the specific test file.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor JavaScript Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to refactor this JavaScript module. Preserve behavior, follow existing Metabase style, and run lint and unit tests.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Cross-File Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-write skill to update all affected TypeScript files. Run formatting, ESLint, type checking, and the most relevant JavaScript or ClojureScript tests.

output_examples.input: Update a Metabase TypeScript component and verify it.
output_examples.output: The agent reviews surrounding component patterns, applies a focused edit, runs type checking, and reports the validation results.

output_examples.input: Fix a JavaScript unit test failure.
output: A failing test is identified or added.
The implementation is changed in a small increment.
The targeted unit test command is run and summarized.

output_examples.output: The agent formats code, runs lint checks, avoids committing, and leaves a concise review summary for the user.

best_practices: Ask the agent to inspect nearby files before changing behavior.
Run the narrowest relevant test first, then broaden checks as risk grows.
Review any Yarn script before allowing it to run in an unfamiliar repository.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for repositories that lack the listed Metabase commands.
Do not ask the agent to skip tests for behavior changes.
Do not let the agent commit changes without separate user approval.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents write and refactor Metabase TypeScript and JavaScript code using local workflow expectations.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill documents commands an agent may use. Execution depends on the tool environment and user permissions.

faq.question: Can it be used outside Metabase?
faq.answer: It may help similar projects, but its commands and standards are written for the Metabase repository.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include code generation templates?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance instead of reusable source code templates.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing changes?
faq.answer: Users should review code diffs, test results, and any project scripts that were executed.
